summary: Refined the M7 UI regression matrix so each locale and viewport scenario runs in its own browser context with its own console/pageerror capture and a configured baseURL. This removes the shared-page init-script ordering hazard while preserving observable-only checks, language-switch persistence, and console-clean assertions.

tests_run.result: Clean working tree before writing the R2 handoff.
Listed active tasks; M7-UI-REGRESSION-MATRIX-001 is in progress.

tests_run.command: node -e JSON parse/shape check for M7-UI-REGRESSION-MATRIX-001

tests_run.result: No whitespace or patch formatting issues.
Focused Chromium Playwright test passed: 1 passed (2.9s).
Playwright chromium suite passed: 22 tests, 22 passed.
Electron security preferences passed; allowed IPC channels are desktop:getRuntimeInfo and desktop:setFullscreen.
format, lint, typecheck, and architecture checks passed.

decisions.result: Each locale scenario now uses a fresh browser context, which prevents init-script accumulation and makes en-US, zh-CN, and system-language inputs deterministic.
The manual browser context now receives the configured baseURL so relative navigation stays aligned with the project Playwright setup.

risks: Chromium-only coverage remains acceptable for this task, but Firefox, Edge, and Windows DPI/localization permutations still belong to the separate platform/resolution matrix task.
requested_action: Review the R2 isolation fix and the recorded validation evidence. Accept if the locale, viewport, console-error, and language-switch persistence coverage remains sufficient.
